results.statement: For any finite set S with n elements, |P(S)| = 2^n
results.lean_code:
theorem a0004 For any finite set S with n elements, |P(S)| = 2^n := by
  -- Count argument or pigeonhole principle
  have h1 : ∀ x, P x := by induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih => simp [ih]
  have h2 : ∀ x, Q x := by sorry
  exact ∀ x, R x sorry
results.natural_proof:
Proof of: For any finite set S with n elements, |P(S)| = 2^n

We prove by counting/pigeonhole.
Observation 1: {observation1}
Observation 2: {observation2}
By combining these, we conclude {conclusion}.

results.statement: A tree with n vertices has n-1 edges
results.lean_code:
theorem e0019 A tree with n vertices has n-1 edges := by
  -- Path, cycle, or connectivity argument
  have h_connected : Graph.IsConnected G := by induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n _ => simp
  have h_path : ∃ p, IsPath p u v := by sorry
  exact ∀ x, R x sorry
results.natural_proof:
Proof of: A tree with n vertices has n-1 edges

By graph connectivity and path properties.
The graph has property {property}.
Consider the path {path_description}.
This implies {conclusion}.

results.lean_code:
theorem e0025 sum(i, i=1..n) = n(n+1)/2 := by
  induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n ih => induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n ih => simp [ih]
results.natural_proof:
Proof of: sum(i, i=1..n) = n(n+1)/2

theorem e0038 P ∨ ¬P (law of excluded middle) := by
  by_contra h
  push_neg at h
  -- Derive contradiction
  have c1 : contradiction 1 := by decide
  have c2 : contradiction 2 := by sorry
  exact absurd c1 (fun _ => c2) sorry
results.natural_proof:
Proof of: P ∨ ¬P (law of excluded middle)

Therefore {conclusion}.
